growth_records.snippet: Table 2: 20-h shake-flask, 40 g/L glucose. IAM1183 consumed 76.93 mM glucose, 2,3-BD 45.72 mM, yield 0.59 mol/mol Glc.

growth_records.medium: Semi-defined 2,3-BD production medium + glucose (5-L fermenter)

up.method: calculated = (222 mM initial [40 g/L] - residual 44.21 mM)/44h = 177.79 mM/44h
reported Table 5

yields.note: Ea-2 shake-flask 2,3-BD yield reached 1.01 mol/mol glucose (in-text)

growth_records.cite: Yan Wu et al. Metabolic Engineering of Enterobacter aerogenes for Improved 2,3-Butanediol Production by Manipulating NADH Levels and Overexpressing the Small RNA RyhB. Frontiers in Microbiology (2021). DOI:10.3389/fmicb.2021.754306. [PMC8531500]
growth_records.snippet: Table 5: 44-h 5-L fermenter. IAM1183 glucose consumption 86.74%, residual 44.21 mM, 2,3-BD 35.04 mM, lactate 34.7 mM, formate 60.36 mM.
growth_records.notes: 5-L fermenter batch, 3 L, 44 h, air 1 vvm (aerobic), 37C, pH 6.5-7.0. Glucose initial assumed 40 g/L=222 mM (same medium as flask). Consumed glucose=222-residual; WT=222-44.21=177.79 mM->4.0407 mM/h; Ea-2=222-19.75=202.25 mM->4.5966 mM/h. Product endpoint concentrations (mM) from Table 5, converted to avg volumetric mM/h by /44h. Specific rates not derivable (no OD/DCW in text). Note: reported % glucose consumption (86.74%) not fully consistent with residual-sugar basis; both reported verbatim.

growth_records.medium: Semi-defined 2,3-BD production medium + glucose (5-L fermenter)

up.method: calculated = (222 mM initial [40 g/L] - residual 19.75 mM)/44h = 202.25 mM/44h

yields.note: Ea-2 shake-flask 2,3-BD yield reached 1.01 mol/mol glucose (in-text)

growth_records.cite: Yan Wu et al. Metabolic Engineering of Enterobacter aerogenes for Improved 2,3-Butanediol Production by Manipulating NADH Levels and Overexpressing the Small RNA RyhB. Frontiers in Microbiology (2021). DOI:10.3389/fmicb.2021.754306. [PMC8531500]
growth_records.snippet: Table 5: 44-h 5-L fermenter. Ea-2 glucose consumption 94.11%, residual 19.75 mM, 2,3-BD 252.06 mM, lactate 6.89 mM, formate 53.63 mM.
growth_records.notes: 5-L fermenter batch, 3 L, 44 h, air 1 vvm (aerobic), 37C, pH 6.5-7.0. Glucose initial assumed 40 g/L=222 mM (same medium as flask). Consumed glucose=222-residual; WT=222-44.21=177.79 mM->4.0407 mM/h; Ea-2=222-19.75=202.25 mM->4.5966 mM/h. Product endpoint concentrations (mM) from Table 5, converted to avg volumetric mM/h by /44h. Specific rates not derivable (no OD/DCW in text). Note: reported % glucose consumption (94.11%) not fully consistent with residual-sugar basis; both reported verbatim.

growth_records.lvlwhy: the source paper (often a review/modeling reuse) states no strain
growth_records.cite: Dethlefsen et al. Differences in codon bias cannot explain differences in translational power among microbes. BMC Bioinformatics (2005). DOI:10.1186/1471-2105-6-3. [PMC546186]
growth_records.notes: Table 1. Temp-corrected-to-37C mu (col3)=0.94 hr-1; actual temp 35C; factor 1.18. Actual mu = 0.94/1.18 = 0.797 hr-1; td=0.870 h. Chemostat: D=mu=0.797 hr-1. rrn copy# ~ (7). Cited from ref [98]; secondary. No uptake/secretion/medium.
